# Introduction

## A Book About Building, Not Just Reading

Most technical books have a dirty secret: by the time you finish them, you still haven't built anything. You've accumulated a lot of knowledge, highlighted a lot of passages, maybe taken some notes — and then you close the book and stare at a blank editor wondering where to start.

This book is different. Or at least, it's trying very hard to be.

Every chapter in *Building Apps with AI for Bunnies* is written with one goal: get you closer to a working, deployed, real-world AI application. Not a toy. Not a tutorial project that only works on your laptop. Something real — something you could show someone, share a link to, or maybe even charge money for.

---

## Who This Book Is For

This book is for you if:

- You've heard everyone talking about AI and want to actually *use* it to build things, not just chat with it
- You've tried to follow coding tutorials but got lost the moment things didn't go as expected
- You have an app idea and you want to stop having it and start building it
- You're a designer, a product manager, a writer, a small business owner — someone who makes things but isn't a software engineer by training
- You wrote some code once, years ago, and you'd like to get back into it but the world has changed a lot

This book is probably *not* for you if you're already a working software engineer looking for advanced ML theory. You'll find it too slow. That's okay — there are other books for you.

---

## What You'll Build

Over the course of this book, you'll build a complete AI-powered app from scratch. Specifically:

1. **A conversational AI chatbot** — customized with your own personality, knowledge, and purpose
2. **A working frontend** — an actual web interface people can use
3. **A deployed, live app** — accessible via a real URL on the real internet

Along the way, you'll also build smaller things: quick prototypes, API experiments, prompt playgrounds. Building small things teaches you faster than reading about big things.

---

## How to Use This Book

**Read it with a laptop open.** Every chapter has exercises and code examples. Do them. Seriously. Reading about swimming doesn't make you a swimmer.

**Don't skip the sidebars.** You'll see four types throughout the book:

> 🐇 **Bunny Tip** — Shortcuts, best practices, and things experienced developers wish someone had told them earlier.

> ⚠️ **Watch Out!** — Common mistakes, gotchas, and things that will bite you if you're not paying attention.

> 📌 **Remember This** — Core concepts worth burning into memory.

> 🔨 **Try It Yourself** — Hands-on exercises. Actually do these. Please.

**It's okay to get stuck.** Getting stuck is learning. When you hit a wall, re-read the last section, search the error message you're seeing, and ask an AI assistant (yes, including the very ones we're building with) to help you debug. The AI tools in this book are also your learning tools.

---

## A Note on Tools (They Change Fast)

Artificial intelligence is moving at a pace that makes the rest of the tech industry look sluggish. The specific model names, API endpoints, pricing tiers, and interface screenshots in this book may be outdated by the time you read it. That's not a bug — it's the field.

The concepts, however, don't change nearly as fast. How to structure a prompt. How to think about context windows. How to connect an API to a frontend. How to handle errors gracefully. These are the things this book emphasizes.

When in doubt, treat this book as the map, not the territory. The terrain shifts; the map helps you navigate anyway.

---

## One Last Thing Before We Start

There's a particular kind of impostor syndrome that hits beginners in technical fields — this feeling that everyone else already knows all of this, that you're behind, that you're not smart enough to be here.

You're not behind. You're at the beginning. Those are different things.

Every experienced developer was once staring at a blank screen with no idea what they were doing. The only difference between them and you is that they kept going when it got frustrating.

You've already done the hardest part: you opened the book.

Let's build something.

---

*Next: [Chapter 1 — AI, Machine Learning, and Why Everyone Is Suddenly Talking About It](./part-1/chapter-01.md)*
